summary.ascii_graph:
*-------------.   Visit each of the following eight Wikipedia pages and extract the two specific facts listed for each topic.  Present your findings as a structured fact matrix (table) with one row per topic and columns for each fact.  Cite every Wikipedia URL.

1. Great Wall of China: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Great_Wall_of_China — Extract: (a) total length in km or miles AND (b) the dynasty that built the most famous sections
2. CRISPR gene editing: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CRISPR_gene_editing — Extract: (a) the protein used as molecular scissors AND (b) the year CRISPR was first used to edit mammalian cells
3. Mariana Trench: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mariana_Trench — Extract: (a) maximum depth in metres or feet AND (b) name of the deepest point
4. Voyager 1: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Voyager_1 — Extract: (a) launch year AND (b) the boundary it crossed to enter interstellar space
5. Rosetta Stone: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rosetta_Stone — Extract: (a) the three scripts on the stone AND (b) the year it was discovered
6. Penicillin: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Penicillin — Extract: (a) who discovered penicillin AND (b) the year it was discovered
7. Aurora (astronomy): https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aurora_(astronomy) — Extract: (a) the scientific name for the northern lights AND (b) what causes auroras (solar particles interacting with …)
8. Enigma machine: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Enigma_machine — Extract: (a) who led the British effort to crack Enigma AND (b) the war in which Enigma was used

After gathering all facts, present a MARKDOWN TABLE with columns: Topic | Fact A | Fact B | Source URL

The
Enigma machine
is a
cipher
device developed and used in the early- to mid-20th century to protect
commercial
, diplomatic, and military communication. It was employed extensively by
Nazi Germany
during
World War II
, in all branches of the
German military
. The Enigma machine was considered so secure that it was used to encipher the most top-secret messages.

rotor mechanism

final_output.final_deliverable:
## Fact matrix (Wikipedia)

| Topic | Fact A | Fact B | Source URL |
|---|---|---|---|
| Great Wall of China | Total length: **“21,196.18 km (13,170.70 mi)”** | Dynasty that built the most famous sections: **“the Ming dynasty (1368–1644)”** (from: “the best-known sections were built by the Ming dynasty (1368–1644).”) | https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Great_Wall_of_China |
| CRISPR gene editing | Protein used as molecular scissors: **“the Cas9 nuclease”** (from: “Working like genetic scissors, the Cas9 nuclease opens both strands…”) | Year first used to edit mammalian cells: **Not present in the provided RAW VISIT CONTENT excerpt** (the captured text is truncated before any explicit year statement about first mammalian-cell editing). | https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CRISPR_gene_editing |
| Mariana Trench | Maximum depth: **“10,984 ± 25 metres (36,037 ± 82 ft; 6,006 ± 14 fathoms; 6.825 ± 0.016 mi)”** | Deepest point name: **“Challenger Deep”** (from the max-depth line: “… (Challenger Deep)”) | https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mariana_Trench |
| Voyager 1 | Launch year: **1977** (from launch date: “September 5, 1977…”) | Boundary crossed to enter interstellar space: **“the heliopause”** (from: “Voyager 1 crossed the heliopause and entered interstellar space…”) | https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Voyager_1 |
| Rosetta Stone | Three scripts: **“Ancient Egyptian hieroglyphs, Demotic script, and Greek script”** | Discovery year: **“Discovered 1799”** | https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rosetta_Stone |
| Penicillin | Discovered by: **“the Scottish physician Alexander Fleming”** (from: “Penicillin was discovered in 1928 by the Scottish physician Alexander Fleming…”) | Year discovered: **1928** | https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Penicillin |
| Aurora (astronomy) | Scientific name for the northern lights: **“aurora borealis”** (from: “The terms northern lights (aurora borealis) …”) | Cause: **Not explicitly stated in the provided excerpt in the requested ‘solar particles interacting with …’ form**; the page states: **“Auroras are the result of disturbances in the Earth's magnetosphere…”** | https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aurora_(astronomy) |
| Enigma machine | Who led the British effort to crack Enigma: **Not present in the provided RAW VISIT CONTENT excerpt** (the captured text does not include the relevant line naming the leader). | War in which Enigma was used: **“World War II”** (from: “It was employed extensively by Nazi Germany during World War II…”) | https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Enigma_machine |

## Verbatim evidence quotes (from the provided RAW VISIT CONTENT)

1) **Great Wall of China** (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Great_Wall_of_China)
- Length: “**Size 21,196.18 km (13,170.70 mi)**”
- Dynasty (best-known sections): “Successive dynasties expanded the wall system; **the best-known sections were built by the Ming dynasty (1368–1644).**”

2) **CRISPR gene editing** (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CRISPR_gene_editing)
- Molecular scissors protein: “**Working like genetic scissors, the Cas9 nuclease opens both strands of the targeted DNA sequence** to introduce the modification…”
- Mammalian-cell year: The provided content is truncated and does **not** include a line stating the year it was first used to edit mammalian cells.

3) **Mariana Trench** (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mariana_Trench)
- Maximum depth + deepest point: “**Max. depth 10,984 ± 25 metres (36,037 ± 82 ft; 6,006 ± 14 fathoms; 6.825 ± 0.016 mi) (Challenger Deep)**”

4) **Voyager 1** (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Voyager_1)
- Launch year: “**Launch date September 5, 1977, 12:56:01** … UTC”
- Boundary crossed: “**Voyager 1 crossed the heliopause and entered interstellar space on August 25, 2012**…”

5) **Rosetta Stone** (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rosetta_Stone)
- Three scripts: “**Writing Ancient Egyptian hieroglyphs, Demotic script, and Greek script**”
- Discovery year: “**Discovered 1799** near Rosetta, Nile Delta, Egypt”

6) **Penicillin** (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Penicillin)
- Discoverer + year: “**Penicillin was discovered in 1928 by the Scottish physician Alexander Fleming** as a crude extract of P. rubens.”

7) **Aurora (astronomy)** (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aurora_(astronomy))
- Northern lights scientific name: “The terms **northern lights (aurora borealis)** and southern lights (aurora australis)…”
- Cause (as stated in provided excerpt): “**Auroras are the result of disturbances in the Earth's magnetosphere caused by enhanced speeds of solar wind from coronal holes and coronal mass ejections.**”

8) **Enigma machine** (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Enigma_machine)
- War: “It was employed extensively by Nazi Germany during **World War II**…”
- British leader: Not present in the provided excerpt.
final_output.action_summary: Produced a Markdown fact matrix for 8 Wikipedia topics using the provided RAW VISIT CONTENT. Extracted exact values and included verbatim supporting quotes for each available fact. Three requested facts (CRISPR mammalian-cell editing year; aurora cause phrased as ‘solar particles interacting with …’; Enigma British leader) are not present in the provided excerpts, so they are marked as missing while still citing the relevant URLs.
